top_signals.title: June inflation falls to 2.6 per cent but analysts say rises ahead
top_signals.summary: Inflation remained stuck above the Bank of England’s target rate despite government promises to tackle the cost of living.  The Office for National Statistics said consumer price index (CPI) inflation was 2.6 per cent in the year to June. Analysts predicted the price growth reading to be 2.7 per cent, lower than the 2.8 per [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: FTSE 100 Live: Stocks to rise; inflation eases in boost for Healey
top_signals.summary: Welcome back to the City AM FTSE 100 liveblog. It’s Wednesday and new Chancellor John Healey secured an early boost after the Office for National Statistics (ONS) revealed that inflation slowed in June. Headline CPI inflation dipped from 2.8 per cent in May to 2.6 per cent in June. Grant Fitzner, chief economist at the [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Customs Modernisation
top_signals.summary: We welcome views on the future of international trade and the impacts of digitalised and modernised practices on the UK customs regime.

top_signals.title: Sony’s 'ironic' PlayStation disc decision upends gamer conventions and threatens a $7 billion resale market
top_signals.summary: In 2013, Sony tweaked Xbox by showing how easy it was to share a PlayStation disc. Now, gamers say Sony has become exactly what it mocked.
